name: Makua Beach
tagline: The last beach before the road ends — remote, wild, and stunningly beautiful with spinner dolphins offshore.
parking: Roadside parking along Farrington Hwy; limited spaces
hours: Open daily, sunrise to sunset; road beyond Makua is gated
bestTime: Summer mornings for calm water and dolphin sightings; avoid after heavy rain
talkStory: Makua means 'parents' in Hawaiian; the valley was considered the birthplace of humanity in Hawaiian cosmology. The U.S. military used Makua Valley for live-fire training for decades, and access to the valley remains restricted, though cultural practitioners hold regular ceremonies. Spinner dolphins are frequently spotted in the bay at dawn. Swimming is only safe in calm summer months — winter surf and currents here are genuinely life-threatening. This is sacred land to many Hawaiians, so treat it with the deepest respect.
